# Installation {#sec-installation}

This chapter walks you through setting up everything you need for computational work in the Horsley Lab. Do these steps in order.

## Step 1: Find Out Your Mac Chip

You need to know whether your Mac has an Apple Silicon chip (M1/M2/M3/M4) or an Intel chip, because you'll download different software versions for each.

Open **Terminal** (search for it in Spotlight with `Cmd+Space`, type "Terminal") and run:

```bash
uname -m
```

- `arm64` → Apple Silicon (M1/M2/M3/M4)
- `x86_64` → Intel

Write this down — you'll need it for the next steps.

::: {.callout-note}
## Windows users
Most of this guide assumes macOS. If you're on Windows, the steps are similar but some commands differ. Where there are differences, both versions are shown. Ask in the lab Discord if you get stuck.
:::

## Step 2: Install R

We use R for all statistical analysis and single-cell work.

1. Go to [cloud.r-project.org](https://cloud.r-project.org)
2. Click **Download R for macOS** (or Windows)
3. Download the version matching your chip (Apple Silicon or Intel)
4. Open the `.pkg` file and follow the installer

To verify it worked, open Terminal and run:
```bash
R --version
```

You should see something like `R version 4.4.x`.

## Step 3: Install Positron

Positron is our coding environment (IDE). It's built for R and Python and has Claude Code built in.

1. Go to [positron.posit.co](https://positron.posit.co)
2. Download the version matching your chip (macOS Apple Silicon, macOS Intel, or Windows)
3. Open the `.dmg` file and drag Positron to your Applications folder (macOS), or run the installer (Windows)
4. Open Positron from your Applications folder

## Step 4: Install Quarto

Quarto is what we use to write analysis documents that combine code, output, and text.

1. Go to [quarto.org/docs/get-started](https://quarto.org/docs/get-started/)
2. Download the macOS or Windows installer
3. Run the installer

## Step 5: Install Claude Code

Claude Code is the AI coding assistant we use in the lab. There are two ways to install it — try Method A first.

::: {.callout-important}
You'll need an Anthropic account to use Claude Code. Ask Valerie if you need help getting access through the lab account.
:::

### Method A: Install via Positron Extensions (Recommended)

This is the simplest method and works directly inside Positron:

1. Open Positron
2. Click the **Extensions** icon in the left sidebar (looks like four squares)
3. Search for **Claude Code**
4. Click **Install**

Claude Code will appear as a panel in the Positron sidebar. This is where you'll use it for all lab work.

### Method B: Install via Terminal

If Method A doesn't work, install via the terminal:

1. First, install Node.js from [nodejs.org](https://nodejs.org) — download the LTS version and run the installer
2. Open Terminal and run:

```bash
npm install -g @anthropic-ai/claude-code
```

3. Verify it worked:

```bash
claude --version
```

::: {.callout-warning}
## `claude` command not found?
If you type `claude` in Terminal and get "command not found", the install location isn't in your PATH. Try:

```bash
npx @anthropic-ai/claude-code
```

If that works, ask in the lab Discord for help adding it to your PATH permanently.
:::

::: {.callout-warning}
## npm errors?
If you get npm errors, make sure Node.js is installed first (`node --version` should return a version number). If Node.js is installed but npm still errors, try:

```bash
npm install -g @anthropic-ai/claude-code --force
```

Or fall back to Method A (installing via Positron Extensions).
:::

## Step 6: Install the Lab Skills

Our lab has custom Claude Code skills that teach Claude our conventions.

### Method A: Install via Positron sidebar (try first)

In the Claude Code panel in Positron, type:

```
/plugin marketplace add https://github.com/Horsleylab/horsley-lab-skills
/plugin install horsley-lab-skills
```

### Method B: Install via Terminal

If the Positron sidebar gives an error, open Terminal, type `claude` to launch Claude Code, then run:

```
/plugin marketplace add https://github.com/Horsleylab/horsley-lab-skills
/plugin install horsley-lab-skills
```

### Method C: Install manually (if Methods A and B don't work)

If the plugin system isn't working, install the skills directly:

```bash
mkdir -p ~/.claude/skills
cd ~/.claude/skills
git clone https://github.com/Horsleylab/horsley-lab-skills.git temp
cp -r temp/skills/* .
rm -rf temp
```

To verify the skills installed, run:
```bash
ls ~/.claude/skills/
```

You should see folders including `audit-script`, `learn-code`, `new-project`, and others.

::: {.callout-tip}
## Skills update automatically
If you installed via the plugin system (Methods A or B), skills update automatically when you restart Claude Code. If you installed manually (Method C), you'll need to re-run the clone commands to get updates.
:::

## Step 7: Install Git

Git is how we track changes to code and collaborate with others.

Check if you already have it:
```bash
git --version
```

If not installed, macOS will prompt you to install it automatically. Follow the prompts. On Windows, download Git from [git-scm.com](https://git-scm.com).

Then configure it with your name and email:
```bash
git config --global user.name "Your Name"
git config --global user.email "your.email@yale.edu"
```

## Step 8: Create a GitHub Account

If you don't have one, create a free account at [github.com](https://github.com). Use your Yale email address.

Then ask Valerie to add you to the [Horsleylab GitHub organization](https://github.com/Horsleylab).

## You're Ready

Once all steps are complete, move on to @sec-first-project where you'll create your first analysis project.

::: {.callout-note}
## Getting Help
If you get stuck at any step, ask in the lab Discord #resources channel or email [valerie.horsley@yale.edu](mailto:valerie.horsley@yale.edu).
:::

---

## Troubleshooting

### `claude` command not found in Terminal

This means Claude Code wasn't added to your PATH during installation. Try:

```bash
npx @anthropic-ai/claude-code
```

If that works, the install succeeded but the PATH wasn't updated. Ask in the lab Discord for help fixing this permanently, or just use Claude Code via the Positron sidebar instead.

### Claude Code not showing in Positron

If Claude Code isn't visible as a panel in Positron after installing:

1. Go to the Extensions panel (**Cmd+Shift+X** / **Ctrl+Shift+X**)
2. Search "Claude Code"
3. Make sure it shows as **Installed** — if not, click Install
4. Restart Positron

### `/plugin` command not recognized in Positron sidebar

Some versions of Claude Code don't support `/plugin` in the sidebar — it only works in the terminal version. Try Method C (manual install) from Step 6 instead.

### Plugin install fails with SSH error

```
SSH authentication failed
```

This means the plugin system is trying to use SSH to clone from GitHub but your SSH keys aren't set up. Use the HTTPS version of the command instead — make sure you're using:

```
/plugin marketplace add https://github.com/Horsleylab/horsley-lab-skills
```

Not:

```
/plugin marketplace add github.com/Horsleylab/horsley-lab-skills
```

### Plugin install fails with "marketplace.json not found"

This is a plugin system configuration issue. Skip to Method C (manual install) from Step 6.

### npm errors during Claude Code install

Make sure Node.js is installed first:

```bash
node --version
```

If you get "command not found", install Node.js from [nodejs.org](https://nodejs.org) first, then retry the npm install.

### Nothing works

Install Claude Code via the Positron Extensions panel (Step 5, Method A) and install the lab skills manually (Step 6, Method C). This combination bypasses all the terminal and PATH issues and works on any machine.
